text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Margaret Yuen
MRN: [REDACTED]
SPECIMEN ID: SP-XXXX-XXXXX
ACCESSION #: [REDACTED]
ORDERING CLINICIAN: Thoracic Oncology
CLINICAL INDICATION: 63-year-old female with known Stage IIIB EGFR L858R-mutant lung adenocarcinoma (left upper lobe primary), previously treated with concurrent chemoradiation followed by Tagrisso consolidation; now with radiographic evidence of new right lower lobe nodule (0.9 cm) on CT chest, suspected disease progression. Biopsy performed for histologic confirmation of progression and assessment of resistance mechanisms.

---

TYPE OF SPECIMEN: CT-guided core needle biopsy, right lower lobe lung nodule
PROCEDURE DATE: [DATE ADDED PROGRAMMATICALLY]
PROCEDURE TYPE: CT-guided percutaneous core needle biopsy (18-gauge, 3 passes)
SITE: Right lower lobe, posterior segment; 0.9 cm nodule
CLINICIAN PERFORMING PROCEDURE: Interventional Radiology

---

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:

RIGHT LOWER LOBE LUNG, CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:

METASTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, CONSISTENT WITH KNOWN PRIMARY PULMONARY ADENOCARCINOMA.

See ancillary studies below.

---

GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Received in formalin are three tan-pink, cylindrical core needle biopsy fragments, each measuring approximately 0.9-1.1 cm in length x 0.1 cm in diameter. The cores are soft to firm, tan-white to pale gray in color, with no grossly necrotic or hemorrhagic areas identified. All three cores are submitted entirely in one cassette following routine processing for hematoxylin and eosin staining and immunohistochemical studies. Touch preparation slides were made at time of procedure and reviewed by on-site cytology; preliminary interpretation was positive for malignant cells, consistent with adenocarcinoma.

Representative sections: 1 cassette, entirely submitted.

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Histologic sections show lung parenchyma with infiltrating malignant epithelial cells arranged predominantly in an acinar and glandular architecture, with focal papillary areas. The tumor cells are intermediate to large in size, exhibiting moderate to marked nuclear pleomorphism, prominent nucleoli, and relatively abundant pale to eosinophilic cytoplasm. Mitotic figures are identified, approximately 4-5 per 10 high-power fields. Areas of central cytoplasmic clearing are noted in a subset of cells. No squamous differentiation, neuroendocrine features, or rosette formation is identified. No definitive micropapillary component is seen in this limited biopsy specimen.

Critically, no histologic transformation to small cell carcinoma is identified. The tumor cells lack crush artifact, nuclear molding, and the characteristic salt-and-pepper chromatin pattern of neuroendocrine differentiation. Synaptophysin and chromogranin immunostains are negative (see ancillary studies).

Tumor necrosis: Focal, comprising less than 10% of the biopsy specimen.

Lymphovascular invasion: Cannot be adequately assessed in core needle biopsy specimen.

Surrounding lung parenchyma: Mild interstitial fibrosis and mild chronic inflammation, changes that may reflect prior treatment effect. No granulomatous inflammation. No fungal organisms identified on PAS or GMS stains.

Background: The histomorphologic features, including the glandular architecture, cytologic characteristics, and immunoprofile (see below), are consistent with metastatic pulmonary adenocarcinoma and are morphologically concordant with the known primary left upper lobe adenocarcinoma diagnosed previously.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES:

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on FFPE tissue sections, appropriate positive and negative controls concurrent):

  - TTF-1 (SP141): POSITIVE, strong diffuse nuclear staining (3+, >90% of tumor cells). Supports pulmonary primary origin.
  - Napsin A: POSITIVE (2+, approximately 60% of tumor cells with granular cytoplasmic staining). Supports lung adenocarcinoma.
  - CK7: POSITIVE, diffuse (3+).
  - CK20: NEGATIVE.
  - p40 (squamous marker): NEGATIVE.
  - Synaptophysin: NEGATIVE.
  - Chromogranin A: NEGATIVE.
  - CD56 (NCAM): NEGATIVE.
  - Ki-67 (MIB-1): Approximately 35% nuclear positivity (proliferative index).

  Interpretation: The immunoprofile is consistent with adenocarcinoma of pulmonary origin. No evidence of histologic transformation to small cell or large cell neuroendocrine carcinoma.

PD-L1 IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
  - Assay: PD-L1 (22C3 PharmDx, Dako/Agilent platform)
  - Tumor Proportion Score (TPS): 5% (tumor cells with partial or complete membrane staining at any intensity)
  - Combined Positive Score (CPS): 9
  - Interpretation: PD-L1 TPS 5% (LOW). Note: this represents a decrease from TPS 15% documented at initial diagnosis biopsy, which may reflect treatment-related immune modulation or sampling variability.

EGFR MUTATION ANALYSIS (PCR-based, Cobas EGFR Mutation Test v2 / confirmatory fragment analysis):
  - EGFR Exon 21 L858R point mutation: DETECTED
  - EGFR Exon 20 T790M point mutation: DETECTED
  - EGFR Exon 19 deletion: NOT DETECTED
  - EGFR Exon 20 insertion: NOT DETECTED

  Interpretation: EGFR L858R sensitizing mutation RETAINED from original diagnostic specimen. EGFR T790M resistance mutation NEWLY DETECTED in this progression biopsy. This pattern of acquired T790M in the setting of progression on osi is recognized, occurring in approximately 10-15% of Tagrisso-resistant cases, and represents a confirmed mechanism of acquired EGFR TKI resistance. Reflexed to comprehensive next-generation sequencing panel (see separate NGS report).

ALK (D5F3 IHC, Ventana): NEGATIVE (consistent with prior testing).
ROS1 (IHC): NEGATIVE (consistent with prior testing).

---

SUMMARY / PATHOLOGIC INTERPRETATION:

The histologic, immunohistochemical, and molecular findings from this right lower lobe core needle biopsy are diagnostic of metastatic adenocarcinoma, consistent with spread from the known left upper lobe primary lung adenocarcinoma. The maintained TTF-1 positivity, pulmonary immunoprofile, and morphologic concordance with the original diagnostic specimen all support this interpretation.

Important findings in this progression biopsy:
1. No histologic transformation to small cell carcinoma -- neuroendocrine markers are negative, ruling out this well-described but uncommon mechanism of EGFR TKI resistance.
2. EGFR L858R sensitizing mutation is retained.
3. Acquired EGFR T790M resistance mutation newly identified, representing a confirmed molecular mechanism of acquired resistance to osi therapy.
4. Decreased PD-L1 TPS (5% currently vs. 15% at diagnosis).

Comprehensive NGS panel reflexed from this specimen for complete resistance mechanism profiling, including assessment for MET amplification, PIK3CA mutations, and other potential bypass pathway alterations.

---

ELECTRONIC SIGN-OUT:
Attending Pathologist: [NAME REDACTED], M.D., Ph.D.
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
Division of Thoracic and Molecular Pathology
[DATE ADDED PROGRAMMATICALLY]

Case reviewed and signed out by attending pathologist. All immunohistochemical controls performed satisfactorily. Molecular results reported separately (NGS report).

---

QUALITY METRICS:
Specimen Adequacy: Adequate for histologic evaluation and ancillary testing.
Total number of cores submitted: 3
Estimated tumor cellularity: approximately 40-50% of submitted tissue.
Tissue submitted for NGS: Yes (FFPE sections and/or macrodissected tumor-enriched cores).

---

DISTRIBUTION: Thoracic Oncology, Interventional Radiology, Molecular Pathology (for NGS reflexing)
